assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSMENT 1:
Safety-net distribution shift is an unaddressed deployment risk. Threshold selection ignores cost asymmetry. HRRP penalty dimension not analyzed. All three require pre-deployment analysis. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSMENT 2:
The three concerns are analytically distinct and all genuine. Safety-net validation gap is the most critical for patient safety. Threshold cost-benefit analysis is feasible pre-launch. HRRP stratification can be run on existing holdout data. The deployment plan as proposed is incomplete but addressable with structured validation conditions. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSMENT 3:
Safety-net extension without validation is a patient safety concern. Capacity-driven threshold ignores the $45/$12,000 cost ratio. HRRP penalty exposure is unanalyzed. These are three genuine deployment governance gaps. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
adjudication_raw: Three of three: empirical_test_agreed. Empirical test specification required for the contested empirical claims.

empirical_test.measure: Safety-net hospital prospective AUC-ROC during pre-extension window; precision at 80th percentile threshold vs. break-even precision for $45/$12,000 cost ratio; HRRP diagnosis-group recall in existing holdout
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC >= 0.68; threshold precision above cost-neutral break-even; HRRP diagnosis groups recall comparable to overall model (within 5 percentage points)
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC below 0.65 or degraded > 10 pp vs. existing hospitals; threshold precision below break-even; HRRP groups show systematic recall deficit
